thread.title: Congress's 3-0 scoreline boosts Karnataka CM Siddaramaiah's image, stuns BJP-JD(S) alliance
thread.title_full: Congress's 3-0 scoreline boosts Karnataka CM Siddaramaiah's image, stuns BJP-JD(S) alliance

author: ManuAiyappa Kanathanda
title: Congress's 3-0 scoreline boosts Karnataka CM Siddaramaiah's image, stuns BJP-JD(S) alliance
text: BENGALURU: Congress on Saturday secured a commanding win in Karnataka bypolls, posting a 3-0 scoreline. The results dealt a big blow to the BJP-JD(S) alliance and boosted chief minister Siddaramaiah's leadership amid corruption allegations.115614684The ruling party retained its stronghold of Sandur and wrested Shiggaon and Channapatna from BJP and JD(S), respectively. Deputy chief minister and state Congress president DK Shivakumar said victory is a further endorsement of Siddaramaiah's leadership and a reflection of people's faith in Cong govt's guarantee schemes. Now, Congress has 138 members (including Speaker) in the legislative assembly, BJ, JD(S) 18 and others two. In a major jolt, third-generation leaders of BJP-JD(S) - Bommai and Gowda families - failed to make an impact. Nikhil Kumaraswamy, son of JD(S) state chief HD Kumaraswamy and grandson of former PM HD Deve Gowda, suffered his third straight electoral defeat. Bharath Bommai, son of Haveri BJP MP and former CM Basavaraj Bommai, lost in Shiggaon in his poll debut.The clean sweep is expected to strengthen Siddaramaiah's position amid ongoing calls for his resignation over alleged irregularities in Mysore Urban Development Authority site allotment and Maharshi Valmiki ST Development Corporation. The results indicate consolidation of Ahinda (a Kannada shorthand for OBCs and Muslims) votes, which Siddaramaiah has been championing. Despite extensive campaigning by BJP and JD(S) on these issues, along with the waqf land controversy, they failed to resonate with voters in these constituencies.The results are a dual victory for Siddaramaiah and Shivakumar, helping maintain a fine power balance within the Congress.Ashok a blames ‘missteps’ for BJP defeatBJP faced internal challenges leading up to the bypolls, with state president BY Vijayendra under fire from party colleagues. Many senior functionaries were missing in action.Opposition leader and senior BJP MLA, R Ashoka, blamed defeat on organisational “missteps” and Congress’s money power and not because of Siddaramaiah or Shivakumar. “The late announcement of candidates for Channapatna hurt our chances, as did defeats of key leaders’ sons,” he said.Channapatna saw a highstakes contest where five-time MLA CP Yogeeshwara, who defected from BJP to Congress days before the election, defeated Nikhil Kumaraswamy by a significant margin. Yogeeshwara’s defection followed a fallout with HD Kumaraswamy, who accused him of aligning with DK Shivakumar and his brother , DK Suresh. “This loss is personal for Kumaraswamy,” said a JD(S) insider, referring to Nikhil’s defeat in a seat represented by his father. In Shiggaon, Congress’ Yasir Ahmed Khan Pathan ousted BJP’s Bharath Bommai, reversing the 2023 assembly election outcome, where Basavaraj Bommai had defeated Pathan.Internal strife also plagued the Congress campaign, with aspirant Syed Azeempeer Khadri briefly rebelling before withdrawing his independent nomination following intervention from party leadership.In Sandur, Congress’s E Annapoorna, wife of Bellary MP Tukaram, defeated BJP’s Bangaru Hanumanthu, a close aide of Vijayendra and former mining baron G Janardhan Reddy. Sandur remains a Congress bastion, with BJP never winning here since its inception.BJP and JD(S) face the challenge of recalibrating their strategies as they gear up for zilla panchayat, taluk panchayat and BBMP polls next year.
